ground_truth.title: AI Gateway Benchmark: Kong AI Gateway, Portkey, and LiteLLM
ground_truth.author: Claudio Acquaviva
ground_truth.main_content: In February 2024, Kong became the first API platform to launch a dedicated AI gateway, designed to bring production-grade performance, observability, and policy enforcement to GenAI workloads. At its core, Kong's AI Gateway provides a universal API to enable platform teams to centrally secure and govern traffic to LLMs, AI agents, and MCP servers. Experimenting with AI is easy, but when it comes to safely and efficiently rolling out AI projects into production, this is a far greater challenge. Kong stands apart by extending its battle-tested API infrastructure to power modern AI workloads. The benchmark tests were executed in AWS using an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) cluster with mocked LLM endpoints using WireMock. The AI Gateways were exposed through a Network Load Balancer and all components ran on c5.4xlarge instances with 16 vCPUs and 32GiB memory. Kong Konnect Data Planes showed a performance increase of over 200% when compared to PortKey, and over 800% against LiteLLM. With all AI Gateways allocating the same amount of 12 CPUs, Kong was over 228% faster than Portkey and 859% faster than LiteLLM. At the same time, Kong had 65% lower latency compared to Portkey and 86% lower latency than LiteLLM. Kong was not just able to consume the hardware resources provided but was also capable of stressing the LLM layer implemented by WireMock, a behavior not observed with LiteLLM or Portkey. Kong unifies API and AI management in a single platform, giving platform teams the control, visibility, and automation they need to scale AI-driven workloads.
with: In February 2024, Kong became the first API platform to launch a dedicated AI gateway, designed to bring production-grade performance, observability, and policy enforcement to GenAI workloads.
The benchmark tests were executed in AWS using an Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) cluster, with WireMock mocking the LLM to expose OpenAI-based endpoints.
Kong Konnect Data Planes showed a performance increase of over 200% when compared to PortKey, and over 800% against LiteLLM.
With all AI Gateways allocating the same amount of 12 CPUs, Kong was over 228% faster than Portkey and 859% faster than LiteLLM.
Kong was not just able to consume the HW resources provided, but was also capable of stressing the LLM layer implemented by WireMock.
